title: Dungeon Optics Laboratory
description: Students explore a dungeon environment transformed into an optics laboratory. They must position, rotate, and configure magical sunglasses lenses at specific stations throughout the dungeon to redirect light beams, learning about reflection and refraction while solving a multi-step puzzle with energy conservation constraints and full undo/redo support.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 that implements an interactive optics education lab inside a dungeon. Load dungeon_warkarma.glb as the main environment and SunglassesKhronos.glb as a manipulable optical lens tool. Camera: use OrbitControls centered on the dungeon interior with constrained zoom (min 3, max 20 units). Lighting: place a bright directional light simulating a magical beam source at the dungeon entrance, plus dim ambient light (intensity 0.15) for atmosphere. Add 5 colored point lights (red, blue, green, yellow, white) at fixed dungeon stations representing beam emitters. Clone the sunglasses model 5 times and place them at default positions near each station. Each lens is draggable via raycasting (mousedown to select, mousemove to reposition on a ground plane, mouseup to release). Right-click rotates the selected lens by 30-degree increments. The system tracks a total energy budget of 100 units shared across all 5 beams (conservation law). Each beam has an energy slider (range 0-40) in an HTML overlay panel, but the sum must never exceed 100. When a lens is correctly positioned and rotated to align with its beam emitter and a target receptor (glowing sphere), that beam activates with a visible line geometry colored to match. Multi-input coupling: adjusting one beam's energy auto-reduces others proportionally if the budget would be exceeded. Implement full undo/redo with Ctrl+Z and Ctrl+Y tracking lens positions, rotations, and energy values as state snapshots (store up to 30 states). Display a HUD showing: beams connected (0/5), total energy used, and undo/redo stack depth. When all 5 beams connect to their receptors with valid energy levels, play a success animation that spins all lenses and flashes the dungeon lights. The interaction requires approximately 10-14 steps: positioning 5 lenses, rotating them to correct angles, and balancing energy sliders across the coupled system.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  totalEnergyBudget: number,         // total energy available across all beams (fixed at 100)
  beamEnergyRed: number,         // energy allocated to red beam (0-40)
  beamEnergyBlue: number,         // energy allocated to blue beam (0-40)
  beamEnergyGreen: number,         // energy allocated to green beam (0-40)
  beamEnergyYellow: number,         // energy allocated to yellow beam (0-40)
  beamEnergyWhite: number,         // energy allocated to white beam (0-40)
  lensesCorrectlyAligned: number,         // count of lenses properly positioned and rotated (0-5)
  activatedReceptors: number,         // count of target receptors receiving beam energy (0-5)
  selectedLensIndex: number,         // index of currently selected lens for manipulation (-1 if none)
  totalRotationApplied: number,         // cumulative rotation in degrees applied to all lenses
  isDragging: boolean,         // whether a lens is currently being dragged
  puzzleCompleted: boolean,         // whether all 5 lenses are aligned and all receptors activated
}
